p: Stop writing spaghetti code for imports.
Laravel Ingest is a robust, configuration-driven ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) framework for Laravel. It replaces fragile, procedural import scripts with elegant, declarative configuration classes.
The system handles the "dirty" work—file processing, streaming, validation, background jobs, error reporting, and API provision—so you can focus on the business logic.

Importing data (CSV, Excel, etc.) is often a painful process: repetitive code, lack of robustness with large files, poor user experience, and inadequate error handling. Laravel Ingest solves this with a declarative, configuration-driven approach.
♾️ Infinite Scalability: Uses Generators and Queues to process files of any size with flat memory usage.
📝 Declarative Syntax: Define what to import, not how to loop over it, using a fluent IngestConfig class.
🧪 Dry Runs: Simulate imports to find validation errors without touching the database.
🔗 Auto-Relations: Automatically resolves BelongsTo and BelongsToMany relationships (e.g., finding IDs by names).
🛡️ Robust Error Handling: Tracks every failed row and allows you to download a CSV of only the failures to fix and retry.
🔌 API & CLI Ready: Comes with auto-generated API endpoints and Artisan commands for full control.
🗺️ Source Agnostic: Import from file uploads, (S)FTP servers, URLs, or any Laravel filesystem disk (s3, local).
🎭 Column Aliases: Support multiple header names for the same field (e.g., ['email', 'E-Mail', 'user_email']).
🚀 Dynamic Model Resolution: Route rows to different Eloquent models based on row data.
Getting started with Laravel Ingest is simple. Follow these steps to integrate the package into your project.
First, add the package to your project's dependencies using Composer:

c: composer require zappzerapp/laravel-ingest

p: Next, publish the configuration file and database migrations. This will create config/ingest.php and the necessary migration files in database/migrations/.
You can choose to publish only the configuration or the migrations by using the tags ingest-config or ingest-migrations.

c: php artisan vendor:publish --provider="LaravelIngest\IngestServiceProvider"

p: Run the database migrations to create the ingest_runs and ingest_rows tables. These tables are essential for tracking the status and results of your imports.
That's it! Laravel Ingest is now installed and ready to be configured.

c: php artisan migrate

p: Let's create a simple importer to understand the core concepts of Laravel Ingest. We will build an importer for a User model.

An importer is a simple PHP class that implements the IngestDefinition interface. This interface has a single method, getConfig(), which returns an IngestConfig object. This object declaratively defines the entire import process.
Create a new file at app/Ingest/UserImporter.php:
This configuration tells Laravel Ingest to:
Import data into the User model.
Expect a file UPLOAD as the source.
Use the email column to identify unique records.
If a user with the same email already exists, UPDATE their record.
Map the CSV column full_name to the name attribute on the model.
Map is_admin to is_admin, transforming the value "yes" into a boolean.
Validate the incoming data using the provided rules.
To make your importer discoverable by the framework, you need to "tag" it in a service provider. The AppServiceProvider is a great place for this.
Laravel Ingest automatically generates a URL-friendly "slug" from the class name. UserImporter becomes user-importer.
Your importer is now ready! You can trigger it via the built-in API or the command line. Prepare a CSV file named users.csv:

c:
full_name,user_email,is_admin
John Doe,john@example.com,yes
Jane Smith,jane@example.com,no

p: Send a multipart/form-data POST request to the auto-generated endpoint:

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <your-api-token>" \
  -F "file=@/path/to/users.csv" \
  https://your-app.com/api/v1/ingest/upload/user-importer

p: If your file is on a disk accessible by the application, you can use the Artisan command:
The import will be queued and processed in the background. You can now monitor its progress.

c: php artisan ingest:run user-importer --file=path/to/users.csv

p: This section covers all aspects of configuring Laravel Ingest, from basic setup to advanced security considerations.
When dealing with file uploads and data imports, security should be a top priority. Here are the recommended security configurations for production environments.

c:
// config/ingest.php
'allowed_file_types' => [
    'csv',
    'xlsx',
    'xls',
    'txt',
],

// config/ingest.php
'max_file_size' => 10 * 1024 * 1024, // 10MB in production

->validate([
    'email' => 'required|email|max:255',
    'phone' => 'nullable|string|max:20|regex:/^\+?[1-9]\d{1,14}$/',
    'price' => 'required|numeric|min:0|max:999999.99',
    'quantity' => 'required|integer|min:0|max:999999',
    'website' => 'nullable|url|max:2048',
    'ip_address' => 'nullable|ip',
])

// config/ingest.php - Production
'allowed_file_types' => ['csv', 'xlsx'],
'max_file_size' => 10 * 1024 * 1024, // 10MB
'log_rows' => true, // Enable for audit trail
'queue_connection' => 'redis', // Use reliable queue
'transaction_mode' => 'chunk', // Safer imports
'chunk_size' => 100, // Smaller chunks for reliability

// config/ingest.php - Local Development
'allowed_file_types' => ['csv', 'xlsx', 'txt', 'json'],
'max_file_size' => 50 * 1024 * 1024, // 50MB for testing
'log_rows' => true, // Enable for debugging
'queue_connection' => 'database', // Simple queue for dev
'transaction_mode' => 'row', // Maximum safety
'chunk_size' => 10, // Small chunks for debugging

// config/ingest.php - Staging
'allowed_file_types' => ['csv', 'xlsx'],
'max_file_size' => 20 * 1024 * 1024, // 20MB
'log_rows' => true,
'queue_connection' => 'redis',
'transaction_mode' => 'chunk',
'chunk_size' => 50,

p: SQL injection prevention measures in place
Rate limiting applied to import endpoints
Data privacy and compliance measures implemented
Security headers and CORS properly configured
Regular security reviews and penetration testing
Remember: Security is an ongoing process, not a one-time configuration. Regularly review and update your security measures to protect against new threats.

c:
# Additional security packages for Laravel
composer require spatie/laravel-security
composer require laravel/fortify
composer require spatie/laravel-activitylog

p: The IngestConfig object is the declarative heart of your importer. It allows you to define the entire ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) process in a fluent, readable way.
All configuration happens inside the getConfig() method of your importer class.
Required. Initializes the configuration for a specific Eloquent model. This must be the static entry point.

c: IngestConfig::for(\App\Models\Product::class)

p: Required. Defines where the data comes from.
$options: An associative array of options required by the specific source handler (e.g., path, disk, url).

c: ->fromSource(SourceType::FTP, ['disk' => 'ftp-disk', 'path' => 'import.csv'])

p: Defines the "Unique ID" column in your source file (not the database column name). This is used to check if a record already exists.

c:
// The CSV has a column "EAN-Code" which is unique
->keyedBy('EAN-Code')

p: Defines behavior when a record with the keyedBy value is found in the database.
DuplicateStrategy::SKIP: (Default) Do nothing. Keep the old record.
DuplicateStrategy::UPDATE: Overwrite the database record with new data.
DuplicateStrategy::FAIL: Stop processing this row and mark it as failed.
DuplicateStrategy::UPDATE_IF_NEWER: Only update if the source data is newer (requires compareTimestamp()).

c: ->onDuplicate(DuplicateStrategy::UPDATE)

p: Used with DuplicateStrategy::UPDATE_IF_NEWER. Compares a timestamp from the source data with a database column to determine if the record should be updated.

c:
->onDuplicate(DuplicateStrategy::UPDATE_IF_NEWER)
->compareTimestamp('last_modified', 'updated_at')

p: A 1:1 copy from source to database. Supports column aliases for files with varying headers.
Transforms the value before saving. Also supports column aliases.
$value: The value of the specific column.
$row: The entire raw row array (useful for combining columns).

l: relate(string $sourceColumn, string $relationName, string $relatedModel, string $relatedKey, bool $createIfMissing = false)
p: If found, assigns the ID to the foreign key of $relationName.
If createIfMissing is true and no match is found, creates the related record automatically.
Synchronizes Many-to-Many relationships from a delimited list in your source data. Perfect for tags, categories, or any pivot table relationship.
$sourceField: Column name in your source file containing the delimited values
$relationName: Name of the BelongsToMany relationship in your model
$relatedKey: Attribute to search for in the related model (default: 'id')
Duplicates in the source list are automatically handled
Non-existing related records will cause the row to fail (unless you handle them in beforeRow())
The entire pivot table for the relationship is synced (existing relationships not in the list will be removed)

c:
// Example 1: Tags from CSV column "Tags" containing "PHP, Laravel, Backend"
->relateMany(
    sourceField: 'tag_names',       // Spalte im CSV (z.B. "Laravel,PHP,API")
    relation: 'tags',                // Name der Beziehung im Model
    relatedModel: Tag::class,        // Klasse des verwandten Models
    relatedKey: 'name',              // Attribut zum Suchen/Aufösen
    separator: ','                   // Trennzeichen (Default: ",")
)

// Example 2: Categories with semicolon separator
->relateMany(
    sourceField: 'categories',
    relation: 'categories',
    relatedModel: Category::class,
    relatedKey: 'slug',
    separator: ';'
)

// Example 3: Multiple role assignments
->relateMany(
    sourceField: 'user_roles',
    relation: 'roles',
    relatedModel: Role::class,
    relatedKey: 'name',
    separator: '|'
)

---

## Validation

### `validate(array $rules)`
Applies Laravel validation rules to the incoming data *before* it is transformed or saved. Keys must match the **source file columns**.

```php
->validate([
    'EAN-Code' => 'required|numeric|digits:13',
    'Price' => 'required|numeric|min:0',
])

p: Merges validation rules defined in the target model's static getRules() method. Useful for DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself). Rules from validate() take precedence over model rules.
Executed before validation. Allows you to modify the raw data array by reference. Perfect for cleaning up messy data globally.
Executed after the model has been successfully saved.
Determines how many rows are processed per background job. Default: 100.
Increase for simple inserts to reduce queue overhead.
Decrease for memory-heavy operations (e.g., image processing in afterRow).
Wraps each chunk in a Database Transaction. If one row in the chunk fails, all rows in that chunk are rolled back.
Overrides the default filesystem disk (from config/ingest.php) for this specific importer.

c: ->setDisk('s3_private_bucket')

p: Enables strict header validation. When enabled, the import will fail immediately if any mapped source column is missing from the file headers. By default, only the keyedBy column is validated.

c:
->strictHeaders()
->map('email', 'email')      // Must exist in source file
->map('name', 'name')        // Must exist in source file

p: Allows you to dynamically determine which Eloquent model to use based on the row data. This is useful when importing heterogeneous data into different tables.
Returns: A fully qualified model class name.
Note: The base model class passed to IngestConfig::for() is used as a fallback if no resolver is set.
Fine-grained control over database transaction behavior.
TransactionMode::NONE: No transactions (default). Each row is committed individually.
TransactionMode::CHUNK: Wraps each chunk in a transaction. Same as calling atomic().
TransactionMode::ROW: Wraps each individual row in its own transaction.

c:
use LaravelIngest\Enums\TransactionMode;

->transactionMode(TransactionMode::ROW)

p: Aktuell unterstützt keyedBy() nur einfache Schlüssel. Für zusammengesetzte Schlüssel (z.B. ['store_id', 'sku']) können Sie Workarounds verwenden:
Erstellen Sie eine kombinierte Spalte in Ihrer Quelldatei:

c:
store_id,sku,product_name,unique_key
1,PROD001,Product A,"1|PROD001"
2,PROD001,Product B,"2|PROD001"

p: Verwenden Sie die beforeRow() Methode, um einen kombinierten Schlüssel zur Laufzeit zu erstellen:

l: Workaround 3: Daten vor dem Import vorverarbeiten
p: Für komplexe Szenarien können Sie die Daten vor dem Import in einem separaten Prozess vorbereiten:
Hinweis: Echte Composite-Key-Unterstützung ist für Version 0.5 geplant und wird diese Workarounds überflüssig machen.
Laravel Ingest provides two configuration files that control the behavior of the package. After installation, publish them with:

c: php artisan vendor:publish --tag=ingest-config

p: The main configuration file for Laravel Ingest.

c:
<?php

return [
    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | API Path
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | The base path for the Ingest REST API endpoints.
    | All API routes will be prefixed with this path.
    |
    | Example: 'api/v1/ingest' => /api/v1/ingest/runs, /api/v1/ingest/upload, etc.
    |
    */
    'path' => 'api/v1/ingest',

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | API Domain
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Optionally restrict the API routes to a specific domain.
    | Set to null to allow all domains.
    |
    */
    'domain' => null,

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Middleware
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Middleware applied to all Ingest API routes.
    | Add authentication middleware here to protect your endpoints.
    |
    | Example: ['api', 'auth:sanctum']
    |
    */
    'middleware' => ['api'],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Registered Importers
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Register your importer classes here with a unique slug.
    | The slug is used to trigger imports via CLI or API.
    |
    | Example:
    | 'importers' => [
    |     'user-importer' => App\Ingest\UserImporter::class,
    |     'product-sync' => App\Ingest\ProductImporter::class,
    | ],
    |
    */
    'importers' => [
        // 'user-importer' => App\Ingest\UserImporter::class,
    ],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Default Chunk Size
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | The number of rows processed per queue job. Can be overridden
    | per-importer using IngestConfig::setChunkSize().
    |
    | - Higher values: Less queue overhead, more memory usage
    | - Lower values: More queue jobs, less memory per job
    |
    */
    'chunk_size' => 100,

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Maximum Rows to Display
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Maximum number of row logs returned by the API when fetching
    | run details. Prevents memory issues with large imports.
    |
    */
    'max_show_rows' => env('INGEST_MAX_SHOW_ROWS', 100),

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Queue Configuration
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Configure which queue connection and queue name to use for
    | processing import chunks.
    |
    | 'connection': The queue connection (redis, database, sync, etc.)
    | 'name': The queue name for import jobs
    |
    */
    'queue' => [
        'connection' => env('INGEST_QUEUE_CONNECTION', env('QUEUE_CONNECTION', 'sync')),
        'name' => env('INGEST_QUEUE_NAME', 'imports'),
    ],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Storage Disk
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | The filesystem disk used for storing uploaded and temporary files.
    | Can be overridden per-importer using IngestConfig::setDisk().
    |
    */
    'disk' => env('INGEST_DISK', 'local'),

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Log Individual Rows
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | When enabled, each processed row is logged to the ingest_rows table.
    | This allows detailed error tracking and retry functionality.
    |
    | Disable for very large imports where row-level tracking isn't needed.
    |
    */
    'log_rows' => true,

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Row Log Retention
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Number of days to retain row logs before they are pruned.
    | The IngestRow model uses Laravel's Prunable trait.
    |
    | To enable automatic pruning, add to your scheduler:
    | $schedule->command('model:prune')->daily();
    |
    */
    'prune_days' => 30,

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Source Handlers
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Maps source types to their handler classes. You can override
    | default handlers or add custom ones here.
    |
    */
    'handlers' => [
        'upload' => LaravelIngest\Sources\UploadHandler::class,
        'filesystem' => LaravelIngest\Sources\FilesystemHandler::class,
        'ftp' => LaravelIngest\Sources\RemoteDiskHandler::class,
        'sftp' => LaravelIngest\Sources\RemoteDiskHandler::class,
        'url' => LaravelIngest\Sources\UrlHandler::class,
        'json-stream' => LaravelIngest\Sources\JsonHandler::class,
    ],
];

p: Max rows returned in API responses
Security-related configuration to protect against malicious uploads and requests.

c:
<?php

return [
    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Maximum File Size
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Maximum allowed file size for uploads in bytes.
    | Default: 50MB (50 * 1024 * 1024)
    |
    | Note: Also check your PHP settings (upload_max_filesize, post_max_size)
    | and web server configuration (client_max_body_size for nginx).
    |
    */
    'max_file_size' => env('INGEST_MAX_FILE_SIZE', 50 * 1024 * 1024),

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Allowed MIME Types
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | List of allowed MIME types for file uploads.
    | Add or remove types based on your import needs.
    |
    */
    'allowed_mime_types' => [
        'text/csv',
        'text/plain',
        'application/vnd.ms-excel',
        'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
        'application/json',
    ],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | Allowed Directories
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | List of allowed root directories for filesystem source operations.
    | This prevents path traversal attacks by restricting file access
    | to specific directories only.
    |
    | Files must be located within one of these directories.
    |
    */
    'allowed_directories' => [
        'ingest-uploads',
        'ingest-temp',
        'data',
        'imports',
    ],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | URL Host Allowlist
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | When set, only URLs from these hosts are allowed for URL source imports.
    | Leave as null to allow all hosts (subject to blocklist).
    |
    | Example: 'example.com,api.example.com'
    |
    */
    'allowed_url_hosts' => env('INGEST_ALLOWED_URL_HOSTS') 
        ? explode(',', env('INGEST_ALLOWED_URL_HOSTS')) 
        : null,

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | URL Host Blocklist
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | URLs from these hosts are always blocked, even if allowlist is not set.
    | Use this to block internal networks, localhost, etc.
    |
    | Example: 'localhost,127.0.0.1,internal.company.com'
    |
    */
    'blocked_url_hosts' => env('INGEST_BLOCKED_URL_HOSTS') 
        ? explode(',', env('INGEST_BLOCKED_URL_HOSTS')) 
        : [],

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | URL Request Timeout
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Maximum time in seconds to wait for URL source requests.
    |
    */
    'url_timeout_seconds' => env('INGEST_URL_TIMEOUT', 15),

    /*
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    | URL Maximum Redirects
    |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    |
    | Maximum number of HTTP redirects to follow for URL source requests.
    |
    */
    'url_max_redirects' => env('INGEST_URL_MAX_REDIRECTS', 5),
];

p: Maximum upload file size in bytes
Always set middleware authentication in ingest.php:
Use URL host restrictions in production:
Restrict allowed directories to only what's needed:
Set appropriate file size limits based on your needs:
c: 'middleware' => ['api', 'auth:sanctum'],
INGEST_ALLOWED_URL_HOSTS=trusted-api.com,data.example.com INGEST_BLOCKED_URL_HOSTS=localhost,127.0.0.1,10.0.0.0/8
'allowed_directories' => [ 'imports', // Only allow imports directory ],
INGEST_MAX_FILE_SIZE=10485760 # 10MB for smaller imports

p: Laravel Ingest uses several PHP 8.1+ enums for type-safe configuration. This page documents all available enums and their values.
Represents the current state of an import run.
Run created but not yet processing

c: use LaravelIngest\Enums\IngestStatus;

PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED
                    ↘ COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS
                    ↘ FAILED

p: Defines where import data comes from.

c: use LaravelIngest\Enums\SourceType;

p: Defines behavior when a record with matching keyedBy value already exists.
Mark row as failed, don't modify existing
Overwrite existing record with new data
Update only if source is newer (requires compareTimestamp())

c: use LaravelIngest\Enums\DuplicateStrategy;

p: Controls database transaction behavior during import.
No transactions, each row committed individually (default)
Wrap each chunk in a transaction
Wrap each individual row in a transaction

c: use LaravelIngest\Enums\TransactionMode;

p: All enums are backed by string values, making them easy to use in database queries:
Use enums in your own code for type safety:

Laravel Ingest is designed to be source-agnostic. You define the source of your data using the fromSource() method on your IngestConfig object. Here are the built-in source handlers and their required options.
This is the most common source type for user-facing imports. It expects a file to be provided via an API request.
Options: No options are required in the IngestConfig.
This handler reads a file directly from one of your configured Laravel filesystem disks (e.g., local, s3). It's ideal for imports triggered by console commands or scheduled jobs.
Payload: The path to the file can be passed as a string payload to the Ingest::start() method (or via CLI --file argument).
path (string): The default path to the file if no payload is provided.
disk (string, optional): Overrides the default disk for this specific import.
This handler downloads a file from a public URL and processes it. The file is streamed to a temporary local file to keep memory usage low.
Payload: None. The URL is configured directly.
url (string): The full URL to the file to be downloaded.

c:
// IngestConfig
->fromSource(SourceType::URL, ['url' => 'https://example.com/data/export.csv'])

p: This handler processes a file containing a JSON array of objects. It's useful for API-driven imports or when dealing with structured data feeds.
Payload: The full path to the JSON file.

c:
// IngestConfig
->fromSource(SourceType::JSON)

// Programmatic trigger
Ingest::start('user-importer', storage_path('app/imports/users.json'));

p: The file must contain a single top-level array. Each element in the array is treated as a row.
These handlers use the RemoteDiskHandler to download a file from a remote server configured as a Laravel filesystem disk. This requires a corresponding disk configuration in config/filesystems.php.
disk (string): Required. The name of the FTP/SFTP disk configured in config/filesystems.php.
path (string): Required. The path to the file on the remote server.
First, configure your disk in config/filesystems.php:
Note: FTP support requires league/flysystem-ftp. SFTP support requires league/flysystem-sftp-v3. Install via:
c: composer require league/flysystem-ftp # for FTP composer require league/flysystem-sftp-v3 # for SFTP

p: Then, use the configured disk in your importer:
You can create your own source handlers for custom data sources (APIs, XML, etc.). See the Custom Source Handlers guide for details.
The Ingest Facade provides a convenient interface to interact with the IngestManager. Use it to programmatically start imports, retry failed runs, and access importer definitions.

c: use LaravelIngest\Facades\Ingest;

p: DefinitionNotFoundException - If importer slug is not registered
If true, validate and transform without persisting
InvalidConfigurationException - If importer config is invalid
Returns: IngestRun - The Eloquent model for the newly created run.
Source data: file path, UploadedFile, URL, or null
SourceException - If source cannot be read
The registered slug of the importer
User to associate with the run (for auditing)
ConcurrencyException - If a retry is already in progress
DefinitionNotFoundException - If importer no longer exists
NoFailedRowsException - If the original run has no failed rows
Retry failed rows from a previous import run.
Returns: IngestRun - A new IngestRun for the retry attempt.
The original run with failed rows
User to associate with the retry run
The retry run maintains a reference to the original:

c:
$retryRun->retried_from_run_id; // Original run ID
$retryRun->parent_id;           // Original run ID

p: Get a specific importer definition by its slug.
The registered slug of the importer
Returns: IngestDefinition - The importer class instance.
DefinitionNotFoundException - If slug is not registered

c: public static function getDefinition(string $slug): IngestDefinition

p: Returns: array - Associative array of slug => IngestDefinition instances.

c: public static function getDefinitions(): array

p: After starting an import, you can monitor its progress, cancel it if necessary, or retry any failed rows. These actions can be performed via Artisan commands or the API.
Each import process creates an IngestRun record in the database. You can query this record to get detailed information about the status and progress of the import.
Use the ingest:status command with the run ID.
This will display a detailed summary, including status, progress counts, and a progress bar for running imports. If the run failed, it will also display the error message.

c: php artisan ingest:status 1

p: Make a GET request to the show endpoint.
The response will be a JSON object containing the full IngestRun details, including a list of all processed rows if the run is configured to log them.

c:
curl -X GET \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/1

p: You can request to cancel a batch that is currently processing. This is useful if you start a large import by mistake.
Note: Cancellation sends a signal to the Laravel Queue batch. It may take a moment for currently processing jobs to finish before the batch is marked as cancelled.

c: php artisan ingest:cancel 1

curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/1/cancel

p: If an import completes with failed rows, you don't need to re-upload the entire file. You can create a new import run that consists only of the rows that failed in the original run.
Use the ingest:retry command with the ID of the original failed run.
This will create a new IngestRun and queue the jobs. You will get a new run ID to monitor.

c:
# Create a new run with the failed rows from run #1
php artisan ingest:retry 1

# You can also perform a dry run of the retry
php artisan ingest:retry 1 --dry-run

p: The response will be the JSON representation of the new ingest run that has been created.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/1/retry

p: For runs with failed rows, you can get an aggregated error summary to quickly identify the most common issues.
The response groups errors by type and shows their frequency:

c:
curl -X GET \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/1/errors/summary

p: Once an importer is defined and registered, you can start an import process in several ways, depending on your use case.
Laravel Ingest provides several Artisan commands for managing imports.
The ingest:run command is perfect for manual or scheduled imports where the source file is accessible on a local or configured filesystem disk.
The slug of the importer to run (e.g., user-importer)
Path to the source file (required for FILESYSTEM sources)
Simulate the import without saving data

c: php artisan ingest:run {slug} {--file=} {--dry-run}

# Run a product import from a file on the default disk
php artisan ingest:run product-importer --file="imports/products.csv"

# Perform a dry run to check for errors
php artisan ingest:run product-importer --file="imports/products.csv" --dry-run

p: List all registered importers and their configurations.

c: php artisan ingest:list

p: Check the status of a specific import run.
The ID of the IngestRun to check

c: php artisan ingest:status {id}

p: Cancel a running import. This will stop processing new chunks but won't roll back already processed rows.
The ID of the IngestRun to cancel

c: php artisan ingest:cancel {id}

p: Retry failed rows from a previous import run.
The ID of the IngestRun to retry
Simulate the retry without saving data

c: php artisan ingest:retry {id} {--dry-run}

p: Clean up old temporary and uploaded files to free disk space.
Delete files older than this many hours
This command removes files from the ingest-temp and ingest-uploads directories that are older than the specified number of hours.

c: php artisan ingest:prune-files {--hours=24}

p: Add to your routes/console.php or scheduler:
To automatically prune old row logs (stored in ingest_rows table), add Laravel's model pruning command to your scheduler:
The retention period is configured via prune_days in config/ingest.php (default: 30 days).

c:
// In routes/console.php or App\Console\Kernel
Schedule::command('model:prune')->daily();

p: Laravel Ingest automatically registers API endpoints to trigger and manage imports. These are ideal for integrations or when providing a user interface for uploads.
To start an import that uses SourceType::UPLOAD, send a multipart/form-data POST request.
Body: Must contain a file field with the uploaded file. You can also include an optional dry_run field set to 1 or true.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -F "file=@/path/to/users.csv" \
  -F "dry_run=1" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/upload/user-importer

p: For importers that don't require a file upload (like FTP or URL sources), you can trigger them with a simple POST request.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/trigger/daily-stock-importer

p: You can start an import directly from your application code using the Ingest Facade. This is useful for complex workflows, scheduled jobs, or custom controllers.
This section covers advanced configuration, optimization techniques, and troubleshooting for Laravel Ingest.
Choosing the right transaction mode and chunk size is crucial for performance when handling large datasets.

c:
# For large imports with more memory
php artisan queue:work --memory=1024 --timeout=600

# For fast processing of small chunks
php artisan queue:work --memory=256 --timeout=60

p: Use URL sources instead of uploads for very large files
Reduce chunk size when encountering memory limit errors
Disable row logging for very large imports:
c: // config/ingest.php 'log_rows' => false,

; php.ini
memory_limit = 1G
max_execution_time = 300
upload_max_filesize = 100M
post_max_size = 100M

p: Der Pfad ist relativ (z.B. imports/file.csv statt /absolute/path/file.csv).
Die Datei existiert nicht auf dem konfigurierten Disk.
Der Disk ist nicht korrekt in config/filesystems.php konfiguriert.
c: ->fromSource(SourceType::FILESYSTEM, ['path' => base_path('storage/app/imports/file.csv')])
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage; Storage::disk('local')->exists('imports/file.csv'); // true/false
// config/filesystems.php 'disks' => [ 'local' => [ 'driver' => 'local', 'root' => storage_path('app'), ], ],
We could not find the file at '/path/to/file.csv' using the disk 'local'.

p: Die CSV/Excel-Datei ist zu groß für das aktuelle memory_limit in PHP.
Zu viele Zeilen werden gleichzeitig verarbeitet (z.B. chunkSize zu groß).
c: memory_limit = 512M
->setChunkSize(500) // Standard: 100
->transactionMode(TransactionMode::NONE)
php artisan queue:work --memory=512
Allowed memory size of 134217728 bytes exhausted

p: Die Spaltenüberschrift im CSV stimmt nicht mit der Definition in map() oder relate() überein.
Groß-/Kleinschreibung oder Leerzeichen unterscheiden sich (z.B. "E-Mail" vs. "email").
strictHeaders(false) setzen (wenn die Spalte optional ist):
CSV-Datei anpassen (z.B. mit Excel oder sed):
c: ->map(['E-Mail', 'Email', 'user_email'], 'email')
->strictHeaders(false)
# Ersetze Leerzeichen in Headern (Linux/Mac) sed -i '1s/ /_/g' input.csv
The column 'user_email' was not found in the source file headers.

p: Die Upload-Zeit überschreitet die PHP max_execution_time.
Der Webserver hat eine geringere Timeout-Einstellung.
Die Datei ist zu groß für den Upload.
c: ; php.ini max_execution_time = 300 upload_max_filesize = 100M post_max_size = 100M max_input_time = 300
client_max_body_size 100M; proxy_connect_timeout 300; proxy_send_timeout 300; proxy_read_timeout 300;
// Für sehr große Dateien const chunkSize = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB chunks // Implementieren Sie chunk-by-chunk Upload
Connection timeout or Request timeout exceeded

p: Der Worker ist abgestürzt oder hat das Zeitlimit überschritten.
c: # Starten php artisan queue:work # Mit Supervisord überwachen php artisan queue:work --daemon --sleep=1 --tries=3

[program:laravel-worker] process_name=%(program_name)s_%(process_num)02d command=php /path/to/your/project/artisan queue:work --sleep=3 --tries=3 --max-time=3600 autostart=true autorestart=true user=www-data numprocs=4 redirect_stderr=true stdout_logfile=/path/to/your/project/storage/logs/worker.log stopwaitsecs=3600
php artisan queue:failed php artisan queue:retry all
Job failed after maximum attempts

p: relate() oder relateMany() kann den referenzierten Datensatz nicht finden.
Die referenzierte ID existiert nicht in der Zieltabelle.
c: ->relate('category_name', 'category', Category::class, 'name', createIfMissing: true)

->beforeRow(function(array &$row) { // Prüfen ob Kategorie existiert if (!empty($row['category_name'])) { $exists = Category::where('name', $row['category_name'])->exists(); if (!$exists) { $row['category_name'] = 'Default Category'; // oder werfen Sie Exception } } })
->relate('category_name', 'category', Category::class, 'name', createIfMissing: true)
SQLSTATE[23000]: Integrity constraint violation: 1452 Cannot add or update a child row

p: Das Datumsformat im CSV entspricht nicht dem erwarteten Format.
c: ->mapAndTransform('import_date', 'created_at', function($value, $row) { if (empty($value)) return null; // Verschiedene Formate versuchen $formats = ['d.m.Y', 'Y-m-d', 'm/d/Y', 'd/m/Y']; foreach ($formats as $format) { $date = DateTime::createFromFormat($format, $value); if ($date !== false) { return $date->format('Y-m-d H:i:s'); } } throw new \InvalidArgumentException("Invalid date format: {$value}"); })
->validate([ 'import_date' => 'nullable|date_format:d.m.Y|date_format:Y-m-d' ])
DateTime::__construct(): Failed to parse time string

p: Import von 10.000 Zeilen dauert mehrere Minuten.
c: ->setChunkSize(1000) // Erhöhen für bessere Performance
-- Index für keyedBy Spalte CREATE INDEX idx_products_email ON products(email); -- Fremdschlüssel-Indizes CREATE INDEX idx_products_category_id ON products(category_id);

// ❌ Schlecht: N+1 Queries ->afterRow(function($model, $row) { $model->category; // Lädt Kategorie für jede Zeile einzeln }) // ✅ Gut: Eager Loading ->afterRow(function($model, $row) { $model->load('category'); })
->transactionMode(TransactionMode::CHUNK) ->setChunkSize(500) // Größere Chunks mit Transaktionen
tail -f storage/logs/laravel.log
tail -f storage/logs/worker.log
// In AppServiceProvider::boot() if (app()->environment('local')) { DB::listen(function($query) { Log::info($query->sql, $query->bindings); }); }
name,email,price Test Product,test@example.com,19.99 Another Product,another@example.com,29.99
php artisan ingest:run product-importer --file=test.csv --dry-run

p: Logs prüfen: Überprüfen Sie Laravel- und Worker-Logs
Konfiguration validieren: Stellen Sie sicher, dass alle Pfade und Berechtigungen korrekt sind
Mit kleinen Datenmengen testen: Isolieren Sie das Problem
Community-Support: Eröffnen Sie ein Issue auf GitHub mit detaillierten Informationen
Für weitere Hilfe besuchen Sie die Dokumentation oder erstellen Sie ein Issue im GitHub Repository.

Laravel Ingest dispatches events at every stage of the import lifecycle. This allows you to decouple your logic: the importer handles the data, and your listeners handle notifications, logging, or cleanup.
Let's implement a listener that sends a summary to Slack when an import completes.

c: php artisan make:notification ImportCompletedNotification
php artisan make:listener SendImportSummaryListener

p: Now, every time an import finishes, the user who started it gets a notification!
Sometimes CSVs aren't enough. You might need to import data from a JSON API, an XML feed, or a Google Sheet. You can add support for any data source by creating a custom Source Handler.
A Source Handler is responsible for one thing: Converting a raw source into a Generator of arrays.
Laravel Ingest doesn't care where the data comes from, as long as you yield it row by row. This ensures memory efficiency even for massive datasets.

l: Tutorial: Building a JSON Array Handler
p: Let's build a handler that accepts a raw JSON array string. This is useful for importing data directly from a frontend POST request body.
Add it to config/ingest.php by mapping an existing SourceType enum value to your handler:
For more flexibility, you can bypass the SourceType enum entirely by using the handler directly in your code:
Here's a more practical example that fetches data from an external API:
Laravel Ingest provides a full REST API, making it easy to build rich, real-time import interfaces in React, Vue, or Livewire.
This guide outlines the standard flow for building an import UI.
Upload: User selects a file. You POST it to the API.
Receive ID: The API returns an IngestRun object with an id and status processing.
Poll: You query the status endpoint every X seconds.
Finish: When status is completed (or failed), you stop polling and show the result.
Here is a simplified example using Vue 3 and Axios.
If run.status is completed_with_errors or failed, you can offer a "Retry Failed Rows" button.
Robust error handling is crucial for a good user experience. Here are comprehensive strategies for handling different types of errors in your frontend.
Before uploading, validate files on the client side:
Different HTTP status codes require different handling:
Create a comprehensive error display component:
Ensure your error handling is accessible:
Validate early: Check files on the client side before uploading
Provide clear feedback: Show specific error messages and actionable suggestions
Handle network issues gracefully: Implement timeouts and retry logic
Be accessible: Use ARIA attributes and screen reader announcements
Log errors: Send error details to your monitoring service
Offer recovery options: Provide retry buttons and alternative actions
Don't overwhelm users: Show only relevant error details and suggestions
Laravel Ingest provides specific exception classes for different error scenarios. Understanding these exceptions helps you implement proper error handling in your application.
Retry attempted with no failed rows
Thrown when there's a problem reading data from a source (file not found, connection failed, etc.).
File does not exist at specified path
URL is unreachable or returned an error
Thrown when attempting to use an importer that hasn't been registered in config/ingest.php.
Solution: Ensure the importer is registered:
Thrown when an IngestConfig is invalid or missing required settings.
Thrown when concurrent operations conflict with each other. Includes factory methods for specific scenarios.
Thrown when attempting to retry an import run that has no failed rows.
Solution: Check for failed rows before retrying:
Thrown during file validation and processing. Includes factory methods for specific file-related errors.
You can handle Ingest exceptions globally in your exception handler:
All Ingest exceptions extend PHP's base Exception class:
This allows you to catch all Ingest exceptions with a single catch block if needed:

c:
Exception
├── SourceException
├── DefinitionNotFoundException
├── InvalidConfigurationException
├── ConcurrencyException
├── NoFailedRowsException
└── FileProcessingException

p: Laravel Ingest exposes a RESTful API for managing and monitoring import processes. All endpoints are prefixed with /api/v1/ingest (configurable via config/ingest.php).
The API routes are wrapped in the api middleware group by default. You will typically need to provide an authentication token (e.g., Sanctum or Passport) in the Authorization header.
Retrieves a paginated list of all ingest runs, sorted by the most recent.
Success Response: 200 OK with a paginated JSON object of IngestRunResource objects.

c: curl -X GET -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest

p: Retrieves the details of a single ingest run, including its rows.
ingestRun (integer, required): The ID of the ingest run.
Success Response: 200 OK with a single IngestRunResource object.

c: curl -X GET -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/123

p: Starts a new ingest run from a file upload.
importerSlug (string, required): The slug of the importer definition (e.g., user-importer).
file (file, required): The data file to import (e.g., CSV, XLSX).
dry_run (boolean, optional): If 1 or true, performs a simulation without saving data.
Success Response: 202 Accepted with the newly created IngestRunResource.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -F "file=@/path/to/data.csv" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/upload/user-importer

p: Starts a new ingest run for a source that does not require a file upload (e.g., FTP, URL).
importerSlug (string, required): The slug of the importer definition.
Success Response: 202 Accepted with the newly created IngestRunResource.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/trigger/daily-stock-importer

p: Sends a cancellation request to a running ingest batch.
ingestRun (integer, required): The ID of the ingest run to cancel.
Success Response: 200 OK with {message: Cancellation request sent.}.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/123/cancel

p: Creates a new ingest run containing only the failed rows from a previous run.
ingestRun (integer, required): The ID of the original run with failed rows.
Success Response: 202 Accepted with the newly created retry IngestRunResource.
Error Response: 400 Bad Request if the original run has no failed rows.

c:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/123/retry

p: Retrieves an aggregated summary of errors for a specific ingest run. Useful for quickly understanding the most common issues without fetching all failed rows.
ingestRun (integer, required): The ID of the ingest run.
Success Response: 200 OK with an IngestErrorSummaryResource object.

c:
curl -X GET \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/123/errors/summary

p: Downloads a CSV file containing only the failed rows from an ingest run. The CSV includes all original data columns plus _error_message and _row_number columns. This enables a "Fix & Re-Upload" workflow.
ingestRun (integer, required): The ID of the ingest run.
Success Response: 200 OK with a streamed CSV file download.
Error Response: 404 Not Found if the run has no failed rows.

c:
curl -X GET \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -o failed-rows.csv \
  https://myapp.com/api/v1/ingest/123/failed-rows/download

p: The JSON representation for an error analysis summary.
The standard JSON representation for an ingest run.
